2017/2018球季意甲賽程：

常見問題：
AC米蘭同國際米蘭又係有"米蘭"，應該點分？
ANS：AC米蘭簡稱米蘭，國際米蘭叫國米，避免稱呼做AC，因為AC係球會咁解，而milan先係名既簡稱。
AC米蘭仲係咪「老人院」？
ANS：現時全隊平均年齡為25歲。
今個轉會窗到目前為止簽咗咁多球員，遲啲仲會唔會有錢買人？
ANS：其實買球員好少會一炮過畀，多數都係分期，所以仲會有錢簽其他球員。
簽咗咁多球員會唔會過唔到FFP？
ANS：早前米蘭管理層有同UEFA探討經營計劃。
http://sempremilan.com/ts-fassone-meets-uefa-to-discuss-ac-milan-business-plan（By 二石四鳥)

梅沙、大$、CONTI就唔好提啦，出都無得點出

Milan consider Politano

Milan are considering Sassuolo’s Matteo Politano as an alternative to Suso, according to reports.

The Rossoneri are looking to plan for next season, with Pepe Reina and Ivan Strinic expected to arrive on free transfers.

Now Tuttosport is reporting that they are also looking at Matteo Politano, who could be an alternative to Suso, or replace the Spaniard if he leaves.

Gennaro Gattuso’s side are eight points away from a Champions League place with seven games remaining, and CEO Marco Fassone admitted in October they’ll have to sell “one or two top players” if they don’t finish in the top four.

Along with Gianluigi Donnarumma, Suso would be one of their most valuable assets.

The newspaper believes Milan will try to lower the cost of the Politano deal by offering players in exchange, with Manuel Locatelli, Matteo Gabbia and Raoul Bellanova potential options.

Politano was close to joining Napoli in January, but the transfer window shut before the paperwork could be completed.

Rossoneri technical director Massimiliano Mirabelli is working hard to strengthen Gennaro Gattuso’s squad and is said to be monitoring the teenager’s progress closely.

The likes of Atlético Madrid, Barcelona, Inter, Juventus and Paris Saint-Germain have already been linked with a move to secure his services, so Mirabelli will probably have to act quickly if he is to get his hands on one of South America’s brightest attacking prospects.

Indeed, the 17-year-old’s contract with the Carioca side contains a €30 million release clause, while it emerged earlier this week that Los Colchoneros have already had a bid of around €14 million turned down by the Brazilians.

Milan give Conti, Romagnoli updates

Milan’s team doctor has given an update on Alessio Romangoli and Andrea Conti, with the former to be evaluated again in “10-12 days”.

Romangoli went off injured in the draw with Sassuolo, and it was confirmed yesterday that he has a thigh injury and won’t be fit to face Napoli this weekend.

“Alessio suffered a muscular strain in the early stages of Sunday’s game,” doctor Stefano Melegati told the Rossoneri’s official website.

“He stopped quickly and the injury turned out to be a small muscular injury, what we’d consider to be a 3A of the first grade, small and with just a few fibres involved.

“It’s an injury which will nonetheless be dealt with very cautiously, as we have important commitments in the near future.

“A new evaluation will be made in 10-12 days, and that evaluation will allow us to be more precise about the recovery time.”

Melegati also gave an update on Conti, who has been forced to undergo a second knee operation, to stabilise the joint after cruciate ligament surgery.

“Andrea is doing really well, both from a clinical point of view and mentally. It’s one week since the operation, things are proceeding well and smoothly.

“It’s of course very early to talk about recovery times, he’s facing various steps on his path to recovery, but I’d say it’s all good.”

Niang to join Nice?

It’s reported that Mino Raiola is trying to move M’Baye Niang to Nice, as he’s not wanted at Torino.

The French forward joined the Granata last summer on loan from Milan with an obligation to buy him outright for €20m in the summer.

According to Tuttosport though, even Walter Mazzarri - who managed Niang at Watford - has lost patience with the 23-year-old, who allegedly regularly goes AWOL from training.

The Coach hasn’t used the forward since giving him half an hour against Fiorentina on March 13, and the newspaper reports that Raiola is working on an exit strategy.

The agent wants to relaunch Niang at Nice, with his client previously having done well in Ligue 1 and Mario Balotelli likely to leave in the summer.

However, Torino have to pay €20m to Milan to sign Niang this summer, so they’ll want to recoup as much of that as possible.

Fassone: 2-3 Milan signings, Gigio…

Marco Fassone reveals Milan fans ‘can expect 2-3 new players in critical positions’ this summer and assures ‘we’re not obliged to sell anyone’.

A lot has been made of Milan’s ownership situation, with President Li Yonghong in the process of refinancing the club, amidst reports that the likes of Gianluigi Donnarumma could be sold if the Chinese businessman fails to raise funds.

Nonetheless, their CEO told Gazzetta dello Sport: “Milan will consolidate their squad.

“80-90 percent of our players have performed at expectations or above them. However, we couldn’t have spent €240m if we hadn’t sold some players or made capital gains on not just players.

“[Sporting director] Mirabelli did a great job. There will now be progressive improvements: the fans can expect 2-3 new players in positions we consider to be critical.

“Sales of key players? With our numbers in hand, on a financial level, I don’t think we’re obliged to sell anyone.

“However, having made a point of the situation with the sporting director and Coach, I can say that there will be players coming in and going out.

“Donnarumma? Everyone has complete confidence in him. He’s one of the pillars and we see Gigio in our minds as our goalkeeper for the long term.”

Fassone: Milan finances ‘very good’

Marco Fassone insists Milan’s finances are ‘very good’ and explains owner Li Yonghong has ‘three options’ to refinance the club.

Fassone’s remarks follow reports that one of Li’s companies were declared bankrupt, just six months before Milan were due to repay a €303m loan to hedge fund Elliott Management.

“Milan are very good from a financial and economic point of view,” the CEO told Gazzetta dello Sport.

“Common opinion can sometimes be deceptively induced so that there seems to be problems.

“Instead, there aren’t any: if we calmly analyse the balance sheets, we can see that Milan aren’t bad at all.

“After that, I’d like to remind you that in buying Milan, Mr Li has indebted himself to the club.

“He made an investment that weighs on only him, lightening the load on the club from a debtor’s point of view.

“The refinancing will happen, even if the modalities are still under discussion. There are three options that Mr Li is evaluating.

“There’s no need for it to be done tomorrow morning, there are still six months and I’m calm enough to believe that it’ll happen on time.

“After that, of course, if the refinancing is for five years and not six months, I wouldn’t need to jump so many obstacles for national and international licenses, but I can live with it.

“The support of the club has always been there and we’ve been allowed to work without ever scraping the bottom of the barrel.

“However, I repeat, the wish isn’t to get to October: if [the refinancing] was completed a couple of months before, maybe four, I’d be calmer.

“In a week to 10 days, a definitive proposal should be with Merrill Lynch.”

Dear AC Milan fans,

I’m very excited to say that today we are celebrating our first-year anniversary with the Club.

Many things happened during the past 12 months. Last summer, we pursued with true passion the most important financial investment in the Club’s history, building the current young, talented and challenging team, led by Rino Gattuso, a passionate and inspirational Head Coach who has my full support, the support of the management team, of all AC Milan people and, I’m sure, of all fans worldwide.

While facing all the challenges along the way, the progress made this year would not have been possible without the commitment of each and every employee. For that, I am very grateful.

I’d take this opportunity to stress once again we are fully committed in this project for AC Milan's growth. Of course, we still have a long way to go to achieve our goal, which is to bring the club back to where it belongs: at the top of European football. While we are celebrating a positive first year, we know this is only the beginning of our project to make AC Milan successful and stable, a project we shall pursue aiming to excellence, and fully respecting all the financial commitments we undertook. Your passion, our passion, will be our daily inspiration.

‘The stars are our winning post’. Thank you all, for your support during the first year of this young and exciting team, together we can achieve something truly special.

Forza Milan!

Cairo: 'Milan asked for Belotti'

Torino President Urbano Cairo confirms Milan asked for Andrea Belotti last season, but “I doubt they’d ask again,” while rejecting offers for M’Baye Niang.

The Granata won the Primavera Coppa Italia for youth teams last night, as Toro beat Milan 1-0 at San Siro.

“I had spoken to the Milan directors last year about Belotti,” Cairo confessed to Sky Sport Italia.

“I didn’t want to sell the player and he didn’t have a release clause valid for Italian clubs. Andrea just had an unlucky campaign, mainly due to injuries, so obviously his numbers aren’t as impressive as last term, but he’s a player I want to relish for next season.

“I doubt they’d ask me for him again.”

Milan did do business with Torino, selling them striker Niang for €14m, who has struggled and slipped down the pecking order.

“He has this injury and can’t play. Niang had some excellent moments, such as when he replaced Belotti as a centre-forward. Perhaps that is his true role, but he can also do well as a winger.

“I think Niang has this enormous potential, which is why in January I turned down offers that were higher than the price I paid for him.”


不了 我好懷疑佢上唔上得返

Suso swap with Callejon?

Suso’s future at Milan seems to be in doubt, with reports of Jose Callejon in a swap deal with Napoli or even a return to Liverpool.

According to different sources, the Spaniard is considering the next step in his career and has a €40m release clause in his contract.

Tuttosport maintain there could be an exchange with Napoli winger Callejon, who is seven years older and whose clause is just €20m.

The newspaper suggests Suso would be more than happy to make the move to Maurizio Sarri’s side, but Callejon would only be prepared to go the other way if Milan were in the Champions League.

It is also improbable because Sarri relies heavily on Callejon, who is very rarely given a rest during a season.

As for Calciomercato.com, they point Suso towards Anfield and what would be a shock return to his old club.

The 24-year-old joined from Cadiz in 2010, but made only 21 appearances for the club over a five-year period before he was sold to Milan for a minimal fee in 2015.

Tottenham Hotspur are also targeting Suso for a potential summer swoop.
